summary: npm package weaponized as CDN-delivered browser redirector to attacker-controlled domain
details: px8my ships px.js, a browser-only script with no Node APIs and no install lifecycle hooks, whose sole purpose is to overlay a host page with a fullscreen loading mask and inject a top-layer iframe (z-index 2147483647) pointing at https://mfmz.dygz123.cn/H.html. The package is not consumable as a Node library and is published to abuse the npm registry plus jsDelivr's CDN (cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/px8my/px.js) as a mutable delivery channel for redirecting browser visitors of any site that embeds the URL. The tarball also includes update_px8my.sh, an operator helper that rewrites the hardcoded redirect URL in px.js to a new domain, bumps the package version, runs `npm publish`, and calls purge.jsdelivr.net to flush the CDN — documenting an active rotation workflow to evade domain blocking while keeping the same npm package as the delivery channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
